title: Integrate container image test scripts into CI
summary: Integrates 8 existing container image test scripts into CI by creating a new verify-container-images.yaml workflow for 5 static tests, adding verify-base-images and verify-service-images jobs to build-images.yaml, replacing the smoke-test job with verify_keystone.sh, adding assert_not_empty to assertions.sh, fixing the stale uv version in verify_venv_builder.sh (0.6.3 → 0.10.8), refactoring verify_keystone.sh to use assert_not_empty, updating verify_build_images_workflow.sh for the new 4-job structure, and documenting the full verification coverage in build-images-workflow.md.

tests_checklist.item: All 5 static test scripts pass (133 total assertions, 0 failures)
verify_keystone.sh uses assert_not_empty instead of manual pass/fail
New test_verify_base_images_job function covers needs, timeout, runner, permissions
Edge cases: test scripts handle yq failures with || echo null fallbacks

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing shell test script PASS/FAIL counter pattern
assert_not_empty follows assert_eq/assert_contains function signature convention
verify-container-images.yaml follows SPDX header and structure convention
Consistent naming: smoke-test → verify-service-images rename applied throughout
No dead code or commented-out code
Functions do one thing, within size limits

security_checklist.item: No direct ${{ matrix.* }} interpolation in run: blocks (expression injection defense)
Verification jobs use packages: read not packages: write (least privilege)
All actions SHA-pinned with version comments
Top-level permissions restricted to contents: read

architecture_checklist.item: Static tests separated from Docker-based tests in different workflows
Dependency chain correct: build-base → verify-base → build-service → verify-service
PR inline step vs push job split maintained for service verification
Solution is minimal — reuses existing scripts without modification (except planned fixes)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — assert_not_empty reuses PASS/FAIL counter pattern

fail_fast_checklist.item: verify-base-images blocks build-service-images (catches base image regressions early)
Test scripts exit 1 on any FAIL count > 0
yq null/empty validation in source-ref and tag derivation steps

defensive_checklist.item: set -euo pipefail in all test scripts
Exit code guard pattern (|| exit_code=$?) used consistently in Docker-based tests

issues_found.description: Local variables still use smoke_* naming (smoke_perms, smoke_timeout, smoke_runner, smoke_fail_fast) despite now referencing verify-service-images job. Naming does not match the entity they reference.
issues_found.fix: Rename local variables to verify_service_perms, verify_service_timeout, verify_service_runner, verify_service_fail_fast. Cosmetic only — does not affect functionality.

issues_found.description: The build-base-images steps table references docker/login-action as v3 and docker/setup-buildx-action as v3, but the workflow file now uses v4 SHAs for docker/login-action. This predates this PR's changes (the table is from CC-0007) and is technically out of scope, but the new verify-base-images and verify-service-images documentation correctly references v4.
issues_found.fix: Out of scope for CC-0028. Could be addressed in a separate documentation sync PR.

issues_found.description: yq binary download does not verify SHA256 checksum. While the URL is pinned to a specific version (v4.45.1) and uses HTTPS, checksum verification would add defense-in-depth against supply chain compromise.
issues_found.fix: Add a checksum verification step after download: echo '<expected_sha256>  /usr/local/bin/yq' | sha256sum -c. Low priority — common pattern in GHA workflows to skip this.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding SHA256 checksum verification for the yq binary download in verify-container-images.yaml (defense-in-depth)
Rename smoke_* local variables in verify_build_images_workflow.sh to verify_service_* for naming consistency
next_steps: Merge PR after approval — all blocking and major checks pass
Monitor first CI run on main to confirm all test scripts execute successfully in the GitHub Actions environment
